content.problemStatement: Consider an undirected graph GGG with NNN vertices, numbered 111 through NNN. You are given an integer sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ describing the edges of this graph — for each pair of vertices uuu and vvv, there is an edge between those vertices if and only if AuA_uAu​ and AvA_vAv​ are coprime. Find the number of connected components of GGG.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer N, the number of vertices in the graph. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN, describing the edges of the graph.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer — the number of connected components in the graph.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are two edges (2,3) and (3,5). There are 3 connected components (1), (2,3,5) and (4).
There are no edges. There are 4 connected components (1), (2), (3), (4).
There are no edges. There are 3 connected components (1), (2), (3).

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a graph traversal algorithm such as Depth-First Search (DFS) or Breadth-First Search (BFS) to find the connected components in the graph. We will first create an adjacency list representation of the graph, where each vertex is connected to all other vertices that have a coprime number. Then, we will use DFS or BFS to traverse the graph and count the number of connected components.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N * M) — where N is the number of vertices and M is the maximum number of edges connected to a vertex. The time complexity is dominated by the graph traversal algorithm.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N + M) — where N is the number of vertices and M is the number of edges in the graph. The space complexity is dominated by the adjacency list representation of the graph.
